questions.q: What three things did Al-Khidr do that Moses could not understand?
opts: A. Healed a sick man, raised the dead, and parted the sea
B. Scuttled a ship, killed a boy, and repaired a wall
C. Turned water into wine, fed a multitude, and walked on water
D. Created a camel from rock, brought rain, and destroyed a city
questions.explanation: Al-Khidr performed these actions to teach Moses about divine wisdom beyond apparent harm: scuttling a ship protected it from seizure, killing a boy prevented future harm, and repairing a wall preserved hidden treasure, showing that God's knowledge encompasses unseen benefits behind events.

questions.q: What did Moses do when the Angel of Death came to take his soul?
opts: B. He slapped the angel on the eye
D. He prayed for more time
questions.explanation: When the Angel of Death came to take Moses' soul, he struck the angel in the eye, causing the angel to return to Allah and request permission to try again. This story highlights Moses' strong will and unique status among the prophets.

questions.q: What miracle is associated with Prophet Ezekiel?
opts: B. Bringing a dead nation back to life by commanding bones to join together
C. Turning a staff into a snake
questions.explanation: Prophet Ezekiel ﷺ is known for the vision where he saw dry bones coming to life, symbolizing the revival of the Israelite nation. This miracle represents God's power to restore life and hope to a seemingly hopeless situation.

questions.q: Why had the people left their homes in thousands according to the story?
questions.explanation: The people left their homes in thousands because they feared dying from a deadly plague spreading among them. This mass departure was driven by their desire to escape the widespread illness and protect their lives.

questions.q: After which prophet was Elisha sent to the Israelites?
questions.explanation: Elisha ﷺ was sent to the Israelites after the prophet Elijah ﷺ, continuing his mission to guide and warn the people. This succession ensured the continuation of prophetic guidance and miracles among the Israelites.

questions.q: According to some sources, what was Elisha's relationship to Elijah?
questions.explanation: Elisha was related to Elijah as his cousin, which is mentioned in some Islamic and historical sources. This familial connection highlights their close relationship and Elisha's role as Elijah's successor in prophethood.

questions.q: Why did the Israelites ask Samuel to appoint a king for them?
opts: A. They wanted to be like other nations
B. To lead them in war against their enemies, the Philistines
C. They were tired of prophets ruling them
D. Samuel was old and dying
questions.explanation: The Israelites asked Samuel to appoint a king because they wanted a strong, centralized leader to unite and lead them in battle against the Philistines, who were threatening their land and safety. This request marked a shift from tribal judges to a monarchy for better military organization.

questions.q: Whom did Allah choose as king for the Israelites?
questions.explanation: Allah chose Saul (Talut) as king for the Israelites because of his strong leadership qualities and physical stature, which were signs of his suitability to lead and protect the people during times of conflict, as mentioned in the Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:247).

questions.q: What sign did Samuel give the Israelites that Saul was their king?
opts: A. He would perform a miracle
B. The Ark of the Covenant would come to them with angels carrying it
D. He would unite the tribes
questions.explanation: Samuel told the Israelites that the Ark of the Covenant would be brought to them, accompanied by angels, as a divine sign confirming Saul as their chosen king, demonstrating God's approval and presence with Saul's leadership.

questions.q: Who did Dawud (David) defeat in single combat?
opts: A. The king of the Philistines
C. A mighty giant from Canaan
D. The leader of the Amalekites
questions.explanation: Dawud (David) defeated Goliath (Jalut) in single combat, demonstrating his faith and courage despite being young and less experienced. This victory is significant as it symbolizes the triumph of faith and righteousness over oppression and tyranny.

questions.q: What weapon did Dawud use to defeat his opponent?
opts: B. A sling and a pebble
questions.explanation: Dawud ﷺ used a sling and a pebble to defeat the giant Goliath, demonstrating that faith and skill can overcome physical strength. This story highlights trust in Allah and courage in facing challenges.

questions.q: What reward did King Saul (Talut) offer to the one who would fight Goliath?
questions.explanation: King Saul (Talut) promised his daughter in marriage as a reward to the person who would defeat Goliath, motivating the Israelites to face the giant warrior. This offer symbolized honor and alliance through marriage, a common practice to strengthen bonds and reward bravery in that time.

questions.q: According to the text, what did Dawud tell Saul about why he was not afraid of Goliath?
opts: A. He had trained in combat since childhood
B. Allah had protected him from lions and bears while shepherding
C. He had a special sword
D. He had seen Goliath in a dream and knew he would win
questions.explanation: Dawud ﷺ explained to Saul that he was not afraid of Goliath because Allah had previously protected him from dangers like lions and bears while he was shepherding, giving him confidence that Allah would also protect him in battle. This trust in Allah’s protection strengthened his courage.

questions.q: What did Allah give Dawud after he killed Goliath?
opts: B. The kingdom and wisdom, and taught him what He willed
C. A long life without enemies
questions.explanation: After Dawud ﷺ killed Goliath, Allah granted him the kingdom and wisdom to rule justly, teaching him knowledge and skills necessary for leadership and judgment, as a reward and sign of His favor. This highlights Allah’s support for His prophets in fulfilling their missions.

questions.q: What book (scripture) was revealed to Dawud?
questions.explanation: Dawud ﷺ was given the Zaboor, known as the Psalms, which is a holy scripture containing songs, praises, and guidance from Allah. It is one of the revealed books mentioned in the Quran, emphasizing Dawud's role as both a prophet and a king.

questions.q: What miracle did Allah grant Dawud regarding nature?
opts: A. The sun stopped for him
B. The mountains and birds joined him in glorifying Allah
C. He could walk on water
D. He could bring the dead back to life
questions.explanation: Allah granted Dawud ﷺ the miracle that the mountains and birds joined him in glorifying and praising Allah, showing the harmony of creation in worship and highlighting Dawud’s special status as a prophet who could communicate with nature.

questions.q: According to the hadith narrated by Abdullah Ibn Amr Ibn Al-As, how did Dawud used to fast?
opts: A. He fasted three days every month
B. He fasted every other day (alternate days)
C. He fasted Mondays and Thursdays
D. He fasted the entire year except for two days
questions.explanation: Prophet Dawud ﷺ used to fast every other day, which is known as the fasting of Dawud. This practice is considered one of the best forms of fasting because it balances worship with physical strength and is highly praised in Islamic teachings.

questions.q: What special quality did Allah give to iron for Dawud?
opts: A. Iron became invisible in his hands
B. Iron became soft and flexible in his hands
questions.explanation: Allah granted Prophet Dawud ﷺ the unique ability to soften and shape iron with his hands, enabling him to craft weapons and tools easily. This miraculous gift demonstrated Allah's support and facilitated Dawud's leadership and justice.

questions.q: Why did Saul become jealous of Dawud?
opts: B. The people praised and loved Dawud, and Saul feared losing his kingdom
C. Dawud married Saul's daughter without permission
D. Dawud wanted to become king immediately
questions.explanation: Saul became jealous of Dawud because the people praised and loved Dawud for his bravery and leadership, which made Saul fear losing his kingdom and authority to him. This jealousy stemmed from concern over his declining influence and power.

questions.q: How did Dawud prove to Saul that he did not want to kill him?
opts: A. He sent a messenger with a peace treaty
B. He crept up to the sleeping Saul, took his spear, and cut a piece of his garment, then awakened him
C. He challenged Saul to single combat and spared his life
D. He returned all the gifts Saul had given him
questions.explanation: Dawud ﷺ showed mercy by taking only a piece of Saul’s garment while he slept, proving he intended no harm. This act demonstrated his respect and innocence, as he could have harmed Saul but chose not to, highlighting his patience and trust in Allah’s plan.

questions.q: How did Dawud earn his living despite being a king?
opts: A. He collected taxes from the people
B. He made and sold weapons (armor)
C. He received tribute from other kingdoms
D. He worked as a shepherd
questions.explanation: Prophet Dawud ﷺ earned his living by making and selling weapons, such as armor, even though he was a king. This demonstrated his humility and dedication to honest work alongside his leadership responsibilities.

questions.q: How did Dawud divide his working day?
opts: A. Two parts: ruling and resting
B. Four parts: earning a living, praying, listening to complaints, and delivering sermons
C. Three parts: worship, work, and family time
D. He spent the entire day in worship only
questions.explanation: Dawud ﷺ divided his day into four parts to balance his responsibilities: earning a living to support himself, praying to maintain his spiritual connection, listening to people's complaints to serve as a just leader, and delivering sermons to guide and educate the community. This division ensured both worldly and spiritual duties were fulfilled.

questions.q: What lesson did the two angels who climbed into Dawud's prayer niche teach him?
opts: B. To always hear both sides of a dispute before passing judgment
C. To be more merciful to criminals
D. To spend more time in worship
questions.explanation: The two angels taught Dawud ﷺ the importance of justice by showing him that he must listen to both parties involved in a dispute before making a judgment, emphasizing fairness and avoiding bias in decision-making.

questions.q: What was the dispute that the two angels presented to Dawud?
opts: A. One man had killed another's son
B. One man had 99 sheep and took the one sheep belonging to his brother
C. Two men were fighting over land
D. A man had stolen money from his partner
questions.explanation: The dispute involved one man who owned 99 sheep and unjustly took the single sheep of his brother, raising a question of fairness and justice. Prophet Dawud ﷺ was asked to judge this case, highlighting his role as a just ruler who resolves conflicts based on truth and equity.

questions.q: How did Dawud initially judge the case of the two angels?
opts: A. He listened carefully to both sides
B. He immediately said the man with 99 sheep had wronged the other
questions.explanation: Dawud ﷺ quickly recognized the injustice when one man claimed to have 99 sheep while the other had only one, showing his wisdom in identifying unfairness and ensuring justice between the two parties. This early judgment demonstrated his ability to discern truth and protect the rights of others.

questions.q: What did Dawud realize after the two angels vanished?
opts: A. That he had judged correctly
B. That they were angels sent to test him and teach him a lesson
C. That he needed to appoint more judges
D. That the case was too complicated
questions.explanation: Dawud ﷺ realized the two angels were sent by Allah to test his judgment and teach him humility and fairness. This experience helped him understand the importance of justice and the divine wisdom behind trials.

questions.q: What did Allah tell Dawud after forgiving him for his hasty judgment?
opts: A. "You are no longer a prophet"
B. "O Dawud! Verily! We have placed you as a successor on earth, so judge between men in truth and justice"
C. "You must step down as king"
questions.explanation: After forgiving Dawud ﷺ for his hasty judgment, Allah reminded him of his role as a just leader on earth, emphasizing the importance of ruling with truth and fairness to maintain justice among people.

questions.q: According to traditions, how many priests mourned Dawud when he died?
questions.explanation: When Dawud ﷺ passed away, it is narrated that four thousand priests mourned him, highlighting the deep respect and sorrow his community felt for him as a prophet and king. This large number reflects his significant spiritual and social influence.

questions.q: What did Sulaiman (Solomon) command to protect Dawud's body from the sun during his funeral?
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ commanded the birds to shade Dawud’s ﷺ body during his funeral to protect it from the sun, showing respect and care for his father even after death. This story highlights the special abilities granted to Sulaiman ﷺ by Allah.

questions.q: What did Sulaiman inherit from Dawud?
opts: B. His prophethood and knowledge (not material inheritance)
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ inherited prophethood and wisdom from his father Dawud ﷺ, rather than material wealth. This spiritual and intellectual legacy enabled him to rule justly and perform miracles, highlighting the importance of knowledge and divine guidance over worldly possessions.

questions.q: What special abilities did Allah grant Sulaiman?
opts: B. He could command the winds and understand the language of birds and animals
C. He could fly without wings
D. He could turn stones into gold
questions.explanation: Allah granted Prophet Sulaiman ﷺ the unique ability to control the winds, enabling swift travel, and to understand the speech of birds and animals, demonstrating his exceptional wisdom and authority over creation as a sign of his prophethood.

questions.q: What did Sulaiman ask Allah for?
opts: B. A kingdom such as none after him would have
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ asked Allah for a unique and unparalleled kingdom to rule justly and wisely, demonstrating his desire for leadership and authority beyond ordinary limits. This request highlights his devotion and responsibility as a prophet and king.

questions.q: What happened when Sulaiman was reviewing his horses and missed the 'Asr prayer?
opts: B. He exclaimed that he loved the good things (horses) more than remembering his Lord, and ordered the horses to be brought back
questions.explanation: When Sulaiman ﷺ missed the 'Asr prayer while inspecting his horses, he realized his attachment to worldly things had distracted him from worship. He expressed regret for loving his possessions more than remembering Allah and immediately called the horses back to prioritize his prayer and devotion.

questions.q: What creatures were gathered in Sulaiman's army?
questions.explanation: Prophet Sulaiman ﷺ was granted the ability to communicate with and command men, jinn, and birds, who formed his army. This unique power allowed him to govern a vast and diverse group of creatures, demonstrating Allah's favor and wisdom bestowed upon him.

questions.q: What did Sulaiman hear when passing through the valley of the ants?
opts: B. An ant warned the other ants to enter their dwellings lest Sulaiman and his army crush them unknowingly
C. The ants were praising Allah
D. The ants were asking for food
questions.explanation: When Prophet Sulaiman ﷺ passed through the valley, he heard an ant warning others to enter their homes to avoid being accidentally harmed by his army, demonstrating his unique ability to understand animal speech granted by Allah.

questions.q: How did Sulaiman react to the ant's warning?
opts: B. He smiled, amused at her speech, and thanked Allah
D. He ordered his army to stop
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ smiled because he was pleased and amazed that the ant could communicate such a warning, showing his wisdom and gratitude. He thanked Allah for granting him the ability to understand the speech of animals, highlighting his unique prophetic gifts.

questions.q: Which bird brought news to Sulaiman about the Queen of Sheba?
questions.explanation: The hoopoe (hudhud) is known in Islamic tradition for bringing Prophet Sulaiman ﷺ news about the Queen of Sheba and her kingdom, demonstrating its role as a messenger and highlighting Sulaiman’s wisdom and communication with animals.

questions.q: What did the hoopoe tell Sulaiman about the people of Sheba?
opts: A. They were preparing to attack him
B. They were ruled by a queen who had a great throne, but they worshipped the sun instead of Allah
C. They were poor and weak
D. They wanted to make a treaty
questions.explanation: The hoopoe informed Prophet Sulaiman ﷺ that the people of Sheba were governed by a powerful queen with a magnificent throne, but they committed shirk by worshipping the sun instead of Allah. This highlights the importance of monotheism and recognizing true divine authority.

questions.q: What did Sulaiman send to the Queen of Sheba?
opts: B. A letter inviting her to submit to Allah as a Muslim
C. A gift of gold and jewels
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ sent a letter to the Queen of Sheba inviting her to submit to Allah and embrace monotheism, demonstrating his role as a prophet who called others to worship the one true God. This letter was a means of peaceful communication and guidance.

questions.q: How did the Queen of Sheba (Bilqis) initially respond to Sulaiman's letter?
opts: B. She consulted her advisors and decided to send a gift to test Sulaiman
questions.explanation: Upon receiving Sulaiman's letter inviting her to submit to Allah, the Queen of Sheba thoughtfully consulted her advisors and chose to send a gift as a test of Sulaiman's intentions and power, demonstrating her cautious and wise approach before fully accepting his message.

questions.q: How did Sulaiman respond to the queen's gift?
opts: B. He rejected it, saying that what Allah had given him was better than any gift
C. He sent it back with a threat
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ rejected the queen's gift to show gratitude and contentment with Allah's blessings, emphasizing that divine gifts surpass worldly offerings. This response highlights his humility and reliance on Allah's favor rather than material wealth.

questions.q: Why did Sulaiman have Bilqis's throne disguised?
opts: A. To confuse her and test whether she would recognize it
D. To make it look more beautiful
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ had Bilqis's throne disguised to test her wisdom and perception, seeing if she could recognize her own throne despite its altered appearance. This demonstrated both his knowledge and the importance of discernment in leadership.

questions.q: What did Bilqis do when she entered the palace with the glass floor?
opts: B. She thought it was water and lifted her skirt, uncovering her legs
D. She immediately recognized it was glass
questions.explanation: When Bilqis entered the palace with the glass floor, she saw her reflection and thought it was water, so she lifted her skirt to avoid getting wet, revealing her legs. This story highlights the clever design of Prophet Sulaiman’s palace and Bilqis’s initial reaction to it.

questions.q: What was Bilqis's final response after witnessing Sulaiman's miracles?
opts: A. She returned to her country to worship the sun
B. She submitted to Allah along with Sulaiman
D. She asked for more time to decide
questions.explanation: After witnessing Prophet Sulaiman's miraculous signs and wisdom, Bilqis recognized the truth of his prophethood and submitted to Allah, embracing monotheism and supporting Sulaiman's leadership. This shows the power of faith and guidance through divine signs.

questions.q: How did Sulaiman die?
opts: B. He died while sitting, holding his staff, overseeing the jinn at work
C. He was killed by the jinn
D. He fell ill and died in his bed
questions.explanation: Sulaiman ﷺ died while seated, holding his staff, as he was supervising the jinn at work. His death was hidden until the staff rotted, revealing that he had passed away without the jinn realizing it.

questions.q: How long did it take for people to realize Sulaiman had died?
opts: B. Many days later, when a worm (ant) ate through his staff and his body fell
questions.explanation: People did not realize Sulaiman ﷺ had died immediately because his body remained standing, propped up by his staff. It was only after a worm (ant) ate through the staff, causing his body to fall, that they understood he had passed away.

questions.q: What lesson was learned from Sulaiman's death regarding the jinn?
opts: B. The jinn do not know the unseen; if they had known, they would not have remained in humiliating torment
D. The jinn can predict the future
questions.explanation: Sulaiman’s death taught that the jinn, despite their powers, do not possess knowledge of the unseen (ghayb). If they had truly known about his death, they would have avoided continuing their labor in humiliation, showing their limited awareness compared to Allah’s knowledge.

questions.q: What did Allah cause to flow for Sulaiman as a miracle?
opts: B. A fount of molten brass (copper)
questions.explanation: Allah granted Prophet Sulaiman ﷺ the miracle of a fount of molten brass (copper) flowing, which helped him build and control his kingdom, including the construction of strong structures and managing his army, showcasing Allah’s power and Sulaiman’s unique blessings.

questions.q: According to Ibn Ishaaq, when did Isaiah (pbuh) appear?
opts: B. Before Zakariyah and Yahya (John the Baptist)
D. During the time of Musa
questions.explanation: Isaiah (pbuh) appeared before Zakariyah and Yahya (John the Baptist) according to Ibn Ishaaq, indicating his prophethood occurred earlier in history. This timeline places Isaiah among the earlier prophets who conveyed God's message prior to the time of Zakariyah and Yahya.

questions.q: Who was the king during Isaiah's time?
questions.explanation: Hezekiah was the king of Judah during the time of Prophet Isaiah ﷺ. Isaiah advised Hezekiah in matters of faith and governance, especially during the Assyrian threat, emphasizing trust in Allah rather than worldly powers.

questions.q: What happened to King Hezekiah while he was sick with an infected foot?
opts: B. King Sennacherib of Babylon advanced towards Jerusalem with sixty thousand men
D. He recovered on his own
questions.explanation: While King Hezekiah was sick with an infected foot, King Sennacherib of Assyria (not Babylon) advanced towards Jerusalem with a large army, threatening the city. This event tested Hezekiah's faith and reliance on Allah for protection and deliverance.

questions.q: What did Isaiah tell King Hezekiah regarding his illness?
opts: A. That he would die soon and should appoint a successor
B. That he would recover immediately
C. That he should flee the city
D. That his enemies would be destroyed
questions.explanation: Isaiah informed King Hezekiah that his illness was serious and that he would die soon, advising him to prepare for his death by appointing a successor to ensure the kingdom's stability. This message emphasized the importance of leadership continuity in times of crisis.

questions.q: How did King Hezekiah respond to the news of his impending death?
opts: B. He turned to the qibla, prayed, wept, and invoked Allah sincerely
C. He became angry at Isaiah
D. He prepared a grand funeral
questions.explanation: King Hezekiah responded to the news of his impending death by sincerely turning to Allah in prayer, facing the qibla, weeping, and earnestly seeking mercy. This shows his deep faith and reliance on Allah’s compassion during a moment of crisis.

questions.q: What was Allah's response to Hezekiah's sincere prayer?
opts: A. He let him die anyway
B. He extended his life by fifteen years and saved him from Sennacherib
C. He gave him more wealth
D. He made him a prophet
questions.explanation: When Hezekiah sincerely prayed for healing, Allah responded by granting him an additional fifteen years of life and protecting Jerusalem from the invading army of Sennacherib, demonstrating divine mercy and power in answer to heartfelt supplication.

questions.q: What remedy did Allah prescribe for Hezekiah through Isaiah?
opts: A. Drink honey mixed with water
B. Extract the water of the fig and apply it to his sore
C. Eat dates and drink camel milk
D. Bathe in the Jordan River
questions.explanation: Allah instructed Isaiah to tell Hezekiah to use the extract of a fig to heal his sore, demonstrating a divine remedy through natural means. This shows how Allah can prescribe both spiritual and physical cures through His prophets.

questions.q: What happened to Sennacherib's army?
opts: B. Allah sent death upon them, and in the morning they were all corpses
questions.explanation: Sennacherib's army was destroyed by a divine punishment when Allah sent death upon them overnight, leaving them all dead by morning. This event demonstrated Allah's power to protect His prophets and punish oppressors.

questions.q: Who was among the survivors of Sennacherib's army?
opts: B. Sennacherib and five of his companions, including Nebuchadnezzar
questions.explanation: Sennacherib and five of his companions, including Nebuchadnezzar, survived the destruction of the Assyrian army as mentioned in Islamic tradition. This event highlights God's protection over certain individuals and the fulfillment of prophetic warnings against arrogance and oppression.

questions.q: According to the Israelite interpolation, how did Isaiah die?
opts: A. He was killed in battle
B. He was sawed in half inside a tree where he had hidden
C. He died peacefully in his bed
questions.explanation: According to some Israelite traditions, Isaiah was killed by being sawed in half while hiding inside a tree, reflecting a martyrdom narrative not found in the Quran but mentioned in certain Jewish and Christian texts. This story emphasizes his steadfastness in faith despite persecution.

questions.q: From which tribe was Jeremiah descended?
opts: B. The House of Levi Ibn Jacob
questions.explanation: Jeremiah ﷺ was descended from the House of Levi, a tribe known for its religious duties and service in the Temple. This lineage highlights his role as a prophet deeply connected to spiritual leadership and guidance in Israel.

questions.q: According to tradition, what did Jeremiah ask Allah?
opts: B. "Which of Your slaves is more lovable to You?"
questions.explanation: Jeremiah ﷺ asked Allah, "Which of Your slaves is more lovable to You?" to understand who holds the highest status in Allah's sight, emphasizing the importance of sincere faith and righteousness in gaining divine love and favor.

questions.q: According to Allah's decree for the Children of Israel in the Scripture, how many times would they do mischief on earth?
questions.explanation: The Children of Israel were foretold in the Scripture to commit mischief on earth twice, reflecting periods of disobedience and moral decline. This serves as a lesson on the consequences of straying from divine guidance and the importance of repentance and reform.

questions.q: Who did Allah send against the Israelites for the first of the two prophesied punishments?
opts: B. Slaves of His given to terrible warfare
questions.explanation: Allah sent the slaves of His who were given terrible warfare as the first punishment against the Israelites because they disobeyed and rejected His commands. This served as a divine consequence to remind them of their covenant and to encourage repentance and obedience.

questions.q: According to Wahb Ibn Munbah, what did Allah reveal to the prophet Amos about the Israelites?
opts: A. That they would be blessed
B. That He would send upon them a tyrannous cruel king with a mighty army
C. That they would conquer the world
D. That their kingdom would last forever
questions.explanation: Allah revealed to the prophet Amos that the Israelites would face punishment through a tyrannous cruel king accompanied by a mighty army, signifying a period of severe trial and oppression as a consequence of their disobedience and wrongdoing.

questions.q: What did Jeremiah do when he received the revelation that Jerusalem would be destroyed?
opts: B. He rent his clothes, applied ashes to his face, fell prostrate, and wept
questions.explanation: Jeremiah expressed deep sorrow and distress upon receiving the revelation of Jerusalem's destruction by tearing his clothes, applying ashes to his face, falling prostrate, and weeping, which were traditional signs of mourning and repentance in his culture.

questions.q: How did the people respond when Jeremiah delivered Allah's warning?
opts: B. They accused him of lying, captured him, tied him up, and imprisoned him
questions.explanation: When Jeremiah ﷺ delivered Allah's warning, many people rejected his message, accusing him of falsehood. They responded harshly by capturing, tying, and imprisoning him, showing their resistance to the divine guidance he brought.

questions.q: Who did Allah send to destroy Jerusalem as punishment?
questions.explanation: Allah sent Nebuchadnezzar (Bukhtanasar), the king of Babylon, to destroy Jerusalem as a punishment for the people's persistent disobedience and rejection of His prophets, including Jeremiah ﷺ. This event served as a divine consequence to encourage repentance and reform.

questions.q: What did Nebuchadnezzar do to the Torah?
questions.explanation: Nebuchadnezzar, the Babylonian king, is historically noted for destroying many religious texts and artifacts during his conquests, including burning the Torah, which was a significant loss to the Jewish community and their religious heritage. This act symbolized the suppression of their faith and culture.

questions.q: What happened to Jeremiah when Nebuchadnezzar found him in prison?
opts: B. He was set free and honored by the king
C. He was taken as a captive to Babylon
questions.explanation: When Nebuchadnezzar found Jeremiah in prison, he recognized his innocence and the truth of his prophecies, leading to Jeremiah being set free and honored. This shows the king's respect for Jeremiah's role as a prophet despite earlier imprisonment.

questions.q: What did Jeremiah advise the remaining weak Israelites to do after the destruction?
opts: B. Repent to Allah and stay in the land
questions.explanation: Jeremiah advised the remaining Israelites to repent to Allah for their sins and remain in the land rather than fleeing, emphasizing patience and faithfulness despite hardships, as this was part of Allah’s plan for their eventual restoration.

questions.q: How did the remaining Israelites respond to Jeremiah's advice?
opts: B. They refused to stay and dispersed throughout the world
C. They repented and were forgiven
questions.explanation: The remaining Israelites rejected Jeremiah's advice to remain and repent, choosing instead to disperse across various lands. This refusal led to their scattering, fulfilling the prophecy of exile and diaspora as a consequence of their disobedience.

questions.q: According to one narration, how long did Jeremiah sleep, and what happened when he awoke?
opts: A. He slept for 100 years and awoke to find Jerusalem rebuilt
B. He slept for 70 years and awoke to find Jerusalem being reconstructed
C. He slept for 40 years and awoke to find his family gone
questions.explanation: Jeremiah is said to have slept for 70 years as a sign of the long exile of the Israelites, and upon waking, he saw Jerusalem being rebuilt, symbolizing the fulfillment of God's promise of restoration after punishment.

questions.q: Who succeeded Laharasab as king and during whose reign did Zoroastrianism appear?
questions.explanation: Bashtaasib succeeded Laharasab as king, and it was during Bashtaasib's reign that Zoroastrianism emerged as a major religion, founded by the prophet Zoroaster (Zarathustra) in ancient Persia.

questions.q: What did Nebuchadnezzar do to Daniel?
opts: A. He made him a minister
B. He threw him into a pit with two lions
questions.explanation: Nebuchadnezzar threw Daniel into a pit with two lions because Daniel refused to stop praying to Allah, showing his steadfast faith. However, Allah protected Daniel by shutting the lions' mouths, demonstrating divine power and mercy.

questions.q: What happened when Daniel was thrown into the lions' den?
opts: A. The lions killed him immediately
B. The lions did not harm him
D. He escaped through a tunnel
questions.explanation: When Daniel was thrown into the lions' den, Allah protected him by preventing the lions from harming him, demonstrating His power and Daniel's faithfulness. This event highlights divine protection granted to the prophets.

questions.q: Who brought food and drink to Daniel in the lions' den by Allah's command?
opts: B. Jeremiah, who was miraculously transported from Jerusalem
questions.explanation: Jeremiah was miraculously transported from Jerusalem by Allah's command to bring food and drink to Daniel in the lions' den, ensuring his survival. This event highlights Allah's protection of His prophets through divine intervention.

questions.q: What did Daniel say when he learned that Jeremiah had brought him food?
opts: B. "Praise be to Allah Who never forgets those who appeal to Him!"
questions.explanation: Daniel expressed gratitude to Allah, acknowledging that He always remembers and responds to those who sincerely call upon Him, highlighting the importance of trust and reliance on God's mercy in times of need.

questions.q: When the Muslim army conquered Tastar, what did they find in the treasure house?
opts: B. A bed with a dead man and a holy scripture beside him
questions.explanation: When the Muslim army conquered Tastar, they discovered a bed with a dead man and a holy scripture beside him, indicating the presence of a revered prophet or holy figure, highlighting the respect for divine knowledge and the connection to prophetic traditions in Islamic history.

questions.q: What was written in the scripture found with Daniel?
opts: B. Life history, annals, songs, speech, and what is to come
questions.explanation: The scripture found with Daniel contained a comprehensive record including life history, annals, songs, speech, and prophecies about future events, reflecting his role as a prophet who documented both past occurrences and divine revelations about what was to come.

questions.q: How long had Daniel been dead when he was found?
questions.explanation: Daniel ﷺ had been dead for 300 years when his body was miraculously found intact, demonstrating Allah’s power to preserve His prophets and signifying the importance of Daniel’s prophethood and message.

questions.q: What was remarkable about Daniel's body after 300 years?
opts: A. It had turned to dust
B. It was preserved except for the hairs of his face (beard and mustache)
D. It had become a skeleton
questions.explanation: Daniel's body remained remarkably preserved for 300 years, with only the hairs of his face (beard and mustache) having decayed. This preservation is seen as a sign of his righteousness and God's protection over His prophet even after death.

questions.q: What was carved on Daniel's ring?
opts: B. Two lions with a man between them, whom they were licking
questions.explanation: Daniel's ring was carved with two lions and a man between them, symbolizing strength and protection. This imagery reflects his role as a prophet who stood firm between powerful forces, emphasizing his courage and divine support.

questions.q: Why did Daniel carve that image on his ring?
opts: B. To remember Allah's blessing upon him when he was saved from the lions
questions.explanation: Daniel carved the image on his ring to serve as a reminder of Allah's protection and blessing when he was saved from the lions, symbolizing his faith and gratitude for divine rescue.

questions.q: What did Ezra see that caused him to wonder how Allah would bring it back to life?
opts: B. A deserted, ruined town with bones
questions.explanation: Ezra saw a deserted, ruined town filled with scattered bones, which made him wonder how Allah could bring such lifeless remains back to life, illustrating Allah's power to resurrect even what seems completely dead or destroyed.

questions.q: How long did Allah cause Ezra to remain dead?
questions.explanation: Allah caused Prophet Uzair (Ezra) to remain dead for 100 years as a sign of His power over life and death, demonstrating that He can revive the dead and control time beyond human understanding.

questions.q: When the angel awakened Ezra, how long did he think he had slept?
opts: B. A day or part of a day
questions.explanation: When the angel awakened Uzair (Ezra), he believed he had only slept for a day or part of a day, highlighting the miraculous nature of his long sleep and God's power over time, as mentioned in the Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:259).

questions.q: What did the angel show Ezra as proof of the long sleep?
opts: A. His donkey had turned to bones
B. His food and drink had not spoiled
D. The town had been rebuilt
questions.explanation: The angel showed Ezra that his donkey had turned to bones to demonstrate the length of his sleep, indicating a significant passage of time. This transformation served as clear evidence that many years had passed during his miraculous rest.

questions.q: What did Ezra say after witnessing the resurrection of the bones?
opts: B. "I know now that Allah is able to do all things"
questions.explanation: After witnessing the resurrection of the bones, Ezra ﷺ realized the absolute power of Allah, affirming that nothing is impossible for Him. This event demonstrated Allah's ability to bring life from death, reinforcing faith in His omnipotence.

questions.q: Who was the only person who recognized Ezra when he returned home?
questions.explanation: When Ezra (Uzair) returned home after many years, only an old maid servant recognized him because she had remained faithful and remembered him, while others had forgotten due to the long passage of time and changes in the community.

questions.q: What miracle did Ezra perform to prove his identity to the maid?
opts: A. He made water flow from a rock
B. He prayed to Allah and she was cured of blindness and lameness
C. He showed her his old clothes
questions.explanation: Ezra ﷺ prayed to Allah, and by Allah's permission, the maid was cured of her blindness and lameness, demonstrating his prophethood and divine support. This miracle confirmed his identity and strengthened the faith of those around him.

questions.q: How did Ezra prove his identity to his son?
opts: A. He showed him a ring
B. His son recognized a black mole between his shoulders that his father had
C. He told him stories from his childhood
questions.explanation: Ezra ﷺ proved his identity to his son by a unique physical mark—a black mole between his shoulders—that only his son knew about, confirming his true identity and ensuring trust between them.

questions.q: What did Ezra do with the hidden copy of the Torah?
opts: B. He copied it out for the Children of Israel from that script
D. He gave it to the king
questions.explanation: Ezra found a hidden copy of the Torah and carefully transcribed it to preserve and restore the sacred text for the Children of Israel, ensuring they had access to their religious laws and guidance. This act helped maintain their faith and identity after periods of hardship.

questions.q: What did the Jews later say about Ezra because of his copying of the Torah?
opts: A. That he was a great scribe
B. That he was the son of Allah
C. That he was a prophet only
D. That he was a king
questions.explanation: Some Jews claimed that Ezra (Uzair) was the son of Allah because they believed his role in copying and preserving the Torah gave him a special, almost divine status, which Islam rejects as attributing partners or offspring to God.

questions.q: What was unique about the people of Yunus?
opts: A. They were the first to worship idols
B. They believed after seeing the punishment, so Allah removed it from them
questions.explanation: The people of Yunus ﷺ were unique because they repented and believed in Allah after witnessing the punishment, demonstrating sincere remorse. As a result, Allah showed mercy by removing the punishment from them, highlighting the power of repentance and Allah’s compassion.

questions.q: Why did Yunus leave his people in anger?
opts: B. They refused to believe despite his warnings
C. He was tired of preaching
questions.explanation: Yunus ﷺ left his people in anger because they persistently rejected his message and warnings to believe in Allah. His departure was due to their stubborn disbelief despite his sincere efforts to guide them.

questions.q: What happened to Yunus when he boarded the ship?
opts: A. He arrived safely at his destination
B. Lots were cast and he was thrown into the sea, then swallowed by a whale
D. The ship sank and he drowned
questions.explanation: When Yunus ﷺ boarded the ship during a storm, the crew cast lots to find who was causing the trouble, and the lot fell on him. He was then thrown into the sea and swallowed by a whale, where he remained until he repented and was saved by Allah's mercy.

questions.q: What did Yunus pray while in the belly of the whale?
opts: B. "La ilaha illa Anta, Glorified be You, truly I have been of the wrongdoers"
C. "Why has this happened to me?"
questions.explanation: Yunus ﷺ prayed, "La ilaha illa Anta, Glorified be You, truly I have been of the wrongdoers," acknowledging God's oneness and his own mistake, which showed his sincere repentance and humility while in the whale's belly. This prayer is a powerful example of seeking forgiveness and relying on God's mercy.

questions.q: How long would Yunus have remained in the whale's belly if he had not glorified Allah?
opts: A. Until the Day of Resurrection
questions.explanation: Yunus ﷺ would have remained in the whale's belly until the Day of Resurrection if he had not glorified Allah, as his remembrance and praise of Allah led to his release. This shows the power of sincere supplication and reliance on Allah in times of distress.

questions.q: What did Allah cause to grow over Yunus when he was cast ashore?
questions.explanation: When Yunus ﷺ was cast ashore after being swallowed by the whale, Allah caused a plant of gourd to grow over him, providing shade and relief during his time of distress, demonstrating Allah’s mercy and care for His prophet.

questions.q: What was Zakariyah's main concern in his old age?
opts: B. He had no children to carry on his work as a prophet and leader
questions.explanation: Zakariyah ﷺ was deeply concerned about having no children in his old age to continue his prophetic mission and leadership. This worry reflects the importance of legacy and succession in guiding the community according to God's message.

questions.q: What did Zakariyah see in Mary's room that amazed him?
opts: C. A light emanating from her
D. A child in her arms
questions.explanation: Zakariyah ﷺ was amazed to see fresh out-of-season fruit in Mary's room because it was a miraculous sign from Allah, indicating divine blessing and the special status of Mary and her child, Prophet Isa (Jesus) ﷺ. This event demonstrated Allah's power to create miracles beyond natural laws.

questions.q: When Zakariyah asked Mary where the fruit came from, what did she reply?
opts: A. "I bought it from the market"
B. "It is from Allah. Verily, Allah provides sustenance to whom He wills without limit"
questions.explanation: Mary explained that the fruit was a blessing from Allah, emphasizing that Allah provides sustenance freely and without restriction to whomever He wills, highlighting divine provision beyond natural means.

questions.q: How did Zakariyah pray to Allah for a son?
opts: B. In secret, as a call in private
questions.explanation: Zakariyah ﷺ prayed to Allah for a son in secret, calling Him privately to show his sincere and humble reliance on Allah's mercy and power, demonstrating deep faith and devotion without seeking public attention.

questions.q: What name did Allah give for the son to be born to Zakariyah?
questions.explanation: Allah named the son to be born to Prophet Zakariyah ﷺ as Yahya (John), who was known for his piety and dedication to God. This name signifies the special status and mission given to him as a prophet.

questions.q: What was unique about the name Yahya?
opts: A. It was the longest name
B. Allah had given that name to none before him
C. It was the name of an angel
questions.explanation: The name Yahya was unique because Allah had not given it to anyone before Prophet Yahya ﷺ, highlighting his special status and the divine wisdom in naming him. This uniqueness signifies his distinct role and mission among the prophets.

questions.q: What sign did Allah give Zakariyah that his prayer would be answered?
opts: A. He would see a star
B. He would not speak to people for three nights, though having no bodily defect
D. He would dream of his son
questions.explanation: Allah gave Prophet Zakariyah ﷺ the sign that he would remain silent for three nights despite having no physical ailment, demonstrating a miraculous indication that his prayer for a child would be granted. This sign emphasized Allah’s power and the certainty of His promise.

questions.q: How did Zakariyah communicate with his people when he lost his speech?
opts: B. He told them by signs to glorify Allah morning and afternoon
C. He remained silent and did not communicate
questions.explanation: When Zakariyah ﷺ lost his speech, he used gestures to convey his message, encouraging his people to glorify Allah morning and afternoon. This method allowed him to continue guiding them despite his inability to speak.

questions.q: What was Yahya commanded to do from childhood?
opts: B. To hold fast to the Scripture (the Torah)
questions.explanation: Yahya (John the Baptist) was commanded to hold fast to the Scripture from childhood, meaning he was to adhere strictly to the teachings and guidance of the Torah. This ensured his purity, wisdom, and dedication to God's message throughout his life.

questions.q: How is Yahya described in the Quran?
opts: B. Sympathetic to men, pure from sins, righteous, and dutiful to his parents
questions.explanation: Yahya (John the Baptist) is described in the Quran as compassionate, sinless, and devoted to righteousness, highlighting his moral purity and strong commitment to his parents, which exemplifies his exemplary character and piety.

questions.q: What was Yahya's diet according to Ibn Wahb?
opts: B. Grass and leaves, and he wept much in fear of Allah
questions.explanation: According to Ibn Wahb, Prophet Yahya ﷺ lived an ascetic life, subsisting on grass and leaves, symbolizing his detachment from worldly pleasures. His frequent weeping reflected his deep fear and consciousness of Allah, highlighting his piety and devotion.

questions.q: Where did Zakariyah find Yahya when he was searching for him for three days?
opts: C. Weeping inside a grave that he had dug for himself
questions.explanation: Yahya ﷺ was found by Zakariyah ﷺ weeping inside a grave he had dug for himself, showing his deep awareness of mortality and the seriousness of his prophetic mission. This reflects Yahya's ﷺ piety and readiness for sacrifice in fulfilling Allah's commands.

questions.q: What did Yahya say to his father about why he was weeping in the grave?
opts: A. "I am practicing for my death"
B. "Did you not tell me that between Paradise and Hell is only a span, and it will not be crossed except by tears of weepers?"
C. "I am sad for the people"
questions.explanation: Yahya ﷺ explained that the distance between Paradise and Hell is very small, and the only way to cross it is through sincere tears of repentance and sorrow. This highlights the importance of heartfelt remorse in seeking forgiveness and attaining Paradise.

questions.q: What did Yahya eat, and where did he sleep?
opts: A. He ate fine food and slept in a palace
B. He ate leaves, herbs, locusts, and slept in mountains or holes in the ground
C. He ate only bread and slept in the temple
D. He fasted continuously and never slept
questions.explanation: Yahya ﷺ lived a simple, ascetic life, eating natural foods like leaves, herbs, and locusts, which symbolize purity and humility. He slept in mountains or holes in the ground to avoid worldly distractions and focus on worship and reflection.

questions.q: What happened when Yahya encountered wild beasts like lions or bears in caves?
opts: B. They recognized him as the prophet who cared for all creatures and left, bowing their heads
questions.explanation: When Prophet Yahya ﷺ encountered wild beasts like lions or bears in caves, they recognized his prophetic kindness and respect for all creatures, causing them to peacefully leave and bow their heads, showing their reverence and submission to his noble character.

questions.q: What unlawful marriage did Yahya condemn?
opts: A. The marriage of a man to his sister
B. King Herod's plan to marry his niece Salome (his brother's daughter)
C. A marriage between a Jew and a Gentile
questions.explanation: Yahya ﷺ condemned King Herod's plan to marry Salome, his brother's daughter, because such a marriage was unlawful in Islamic law due to close blood relations. This stance reflects the importance of adhering to clear ethical and legal boundaries in family relationships.

questions.q: How did Salome cause Yahya's death?
opts: B. She danced for Herod and requested Yahya's head as a reward
questions.explanation: Salome danced before King Herod, pleasing him so much that he promised to grant her any wish. Prompted by her mother, she requested the head of Yahya (John the Baptist), leading to his execution.

questions.q: How was Yahya executed?
opts: B. He was beheaded, and his head was brought to Salome
questions.explanation: Yahya (John the Baptist) was executed by beheading as ordered by King Herod. His head was then presented to Salome, fulfilling her request made during a dance, as described in Islamic and biblical traditions.

questions.q: Who was the father of Mary (Maryam)?
questions.explanation: Mary's father was Imran, as mentioned in the Quran (Surah Al-Imran), which highlights the noble lineage of Maryam and her family, emphasizing their piety and devotion to Allah.

questions.q: What did the wife of Imran vow to Allah?
opts: B. To dedicate the child in her womb to Allah's service (in the temple)
C. To give all her wealth to charity
D. To fast every Monday and Thursday
questions.explanation: The wife of Imran vowed to dedicate her unborn child to Allah's service, meaning the child would be raised in the temple to serve God. This vow was fulfilled with the birth of Maryam (Mary), who was honored for her piety and chosen status.

questions.q: What name did the wife of Imran give to her daughter?
questions.explanation: The wife of Imran named her daughter Maryam (Mary) as mentioned in the Quran, highlighting her special status and purity. Maryam is revered as the mother of Prophet Isa (Jesus) ﷺ and is honored for her devotion and piety.

questions.q: Who became the guardian of Mary?
questions.explanation: Zakariyah (Prophet Zechariah) was appointed as the guardian of Mary (Maryam) to care for her and protect her in the temple, as mentioned in Islamic tradition and the Quran. This guardianship ensured her safety and spiritual upbringing.

questions.q: How was Zakariyah chosen as Mary's guardian?
opts: B. Lots were cast by throwing reeds into the river, and Zakariyah's reed remained afloat
questions.explanation: Zakariyah was chosen as Mary's guardian through a divine sign where reeds were cast into the river, and the reed belonging to Zakariyah remained afloat, indicating Allah's selection of him for this honorable responsibility.

questions.q: How did the angel appear to Mary when he came to announce the birth of Jesus?
opts: B. In the form of a man in all respects
D. As a voice without form
questions.explanation: The angel appeared to Mary in the form of a man to deliver the message clearly and gently, making the divine announcement more understandable and less frightening. This human-like appearance helped Mary comprehend the miraculous news of Jesus’ birth in a relatable way.

questions.q: What was Mary's reaction when she saw the angel?
opts: B. She sought refuge in Allah from him, thinking he might harm her
questions.explanation: Mary sought refuge in Allah because seeing an angel was an extraordinary and overwhelming experience, and she feared it might bring harm. This reaction shows her piety and awareness of seeking Allah’s protection in moments of fear or uncertainty.

questions.q: Where did Mary give birth to Jesus?
opts: A. In the temple in Jerusalem
B. In Nazareth, by a dry palm tree
questions.explanation: Mary gave birth to Jesus near a dry palm tree outside Nazareth, as described in the Quran (Surah Maryam 19:22-26), where she was comforted and provided with sustenance during the difficult birth. This location highlights her solitude and reliance on God's support.

questions.q: What did Mary wish for during the pains of childbirth?
opts: A. That her mother was with her
B. "Would that I had died before this, and had been forgotten and out of sight!"
C. That she had never conceived
questions.explanation: During the pains of childbirth, Mary expressed a wish to have died and been forgotten, reflecting the intense physical and emotional hardship she was experiencing, as mentioned in the Quran (Surah Maryam 19:23). This highlights her human vulnerability despite her honored status.

questions.q: How did Allah provide for Mary during childbirth?
opts: B. A voice told her to shake the palm tree for dates, and a stream of water appeared
D. The people of the town helped her
questions.explanation: During childbirth, Allah provided for Mary by instructing her through a voice to shake the palm tree, causing fresh dates to fall for nourishment, and a stream of water to appear nearby, ensuring she had food and drink in her time of need.

questions.q: How did Mary answer the people who accused her of sin when she brought baby Jesus?
opts: B. She pointed to the baby, and Jesus spoke from the cradle
questions.explanation: When Mary was accused of sin for bringing baby Jesus, she remained silent and pointed to him. Then, Jesus miraculously spoke from the cradle, declaring his prophethood and innocence, which proved Mary's purity and God's miraculous power.

questions.q: What did baby Jesus say when he spoke from the cradle?
opts: A. "I am the son of God"
B. "I am a slave of Allah. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet"
D. "I have come to save you"
questions.explanation: Baby Jesus spoke from the cradle to declare his prophethood and submission to Allah, saying, "I am a slave of Allah. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet," which affirmed his divine mission and innocence from false accusations. This miracle showed his special status among prophets.

questions.q: What did Jesus say about his relationship with his mother?
opts: A. "She is not my real mother"
B. "Allah has made me dutiful towards she who bore me"
D. "She is the Queen of Heaven"
questions.explanation: Jesus emphasized his respect and dutifulness towards his mother, Mary, highlighting the importance of honoring one's parents as commanded in Islam. This shows his obedience to Allah's guidance in maintaining strong family ties.

questions.q: What miracle did Jesus perform with clay birds?
opts: A. He made them fly permanently
B. He made the figure of a bird, breathed into it, and it became a real bird by Allah's permission
C. He turned them to gold
questions.explanation: Jesus ﷺ shaped clay into birds and breathed life into them, and by Allah's permission, they became real birds. This miracle demonstrated Allah's power working through His prophet, showing that Jesus ﷺ had divine support in performing extraordinary acts.

questions.q: What other miracles did Jesus perform by Allah's permission?
opts: B. Healing the blind and lepers, and bringing the dead to life
C. Turning a staff into a snake
questions.explanation: Jesus ﷺ performed miracles such as healing the blind and lepers and bringing the dead back to life by Allah's permission, demonstrating Allah's power and confirming his prophethood. These acts were signs to the people, showing divine support and mercy through him.

questions.q: Who were the helpers of Jesus in Allah's cause?
questions.explanation: The helpers of Jesus (Isa ﷺ) in Allah's cause were known as the disciples (Hawariyyun), who supported him in spreading his message and stood by him during his mission. They are mentioned in the Quran as devoted followers who believed in his teachings.

questions.q: What did the disciples ask Jesus to request from Allah?
opts: B. A table spread with food from heaven
questions.explanation: The disciples asked Jesus ﷺ to request a table spread with food from heaven as a sign of Allah's mercy and to strengthen their faith. This miraculous provision was meant to reassure them of Allah’s power and blessings.

questions.q: What warning did Allah give when He agreed to send down the table?
opts: A. "You must fast for a month"
B. "If any of you disbelieves after this, I will punish him with a torment such as I have not inflicted on anyone among all mankind and jinn"
questions.explanation: Allah warned that if anyone disbelieved after the table was sent down, they would face a severe punishment unlike any previously experienced by humans or jinn. This emphasizes the seriousness of rejecting clear signs and blessings from Allah.

questions.q: According to the Quran, what will Allah ask Jesus on the Day of Resurrection?
opts: A. "Why did you claim to be God?"
B. "Did you say unto men: 'Worship me and my mother as two gods besides Allah?'"
C. "How many miracles did you perform?"
D. "Why did you not fight your enemies?"
questions.explanation: Allah will ask Jesus if he told people to worship him and his mother as gods besides Allah to clarify that Jesus never claimed divinity, emphasizing the strict monotheism in Islam and rejecting any form of shirk (associating partners with Allah).

questions.q: How will Jesus respond to Allah's question on the Day of Resurrection?
opts: B. He will say: "Glory be to You! It was not for me to say what I had no right to say"
D. He will blame his followers
questions.explanation: On the Day of Resurrection, Prophet Isa ﷺ will clarify that he only conveyed Allah's message and did not claim any divine authority himself, emphasizing his role as a servant and messenger, not as someone who spoke on his own behalf.

questions.q: What did Jesus tell his followers?
opts: B. "Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord"
questions.explanation: Jesus ﷺ taught his followers to worship Allah alone, emphasizing monotheism and submission to the one true God, as he himself was a messenger of Allah. This message reinforces the core Islamic belief in the oneness of God (Tawhid).

questions.q: What did the Jews claim about Jesus?
opts: A. That he was a great prophet
B. That they killed him and crucified him
C. That he was an angel
questions.explanation: The Jews claimed that they killed and crucified Jesus, but Islamic teachings clarify that Jesus was not killed or crucified; instead, it appeared so to them, and Allah raised him up to Himself. This belief distinguishes Islamic understanding from the Christian narrative.

questions.q: What does the Quran say actually happened to Jesus?
opts: A. He was crucified and died
B. He was not killed nor crucified, but it was made to appear so to them, and Allah raised him up to Himself
D. He died naturally and was buried
questions.explanation: The Quran states that Jesus was neither killed nor crucified; instead, it was made to appear so to the people, and Allah raised him up to Himself. This emphasizes that Jesus was saved from harm and honored by God, differing from the Christian belief of crucifixion and death.

questions.q: According to the Quran, when will the People of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) believe in Jesus?
opts: A. They already believe in him
B. Before his death (at the time of the appearance of the angel of death) and on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness against them
D. Only after they convert to Islam
questions.explanation: The Quran states that the People of the Scripture will believe in Jesus ﷺ before his death when the angel of death approaches him, and on the Day of Resurrection, Jesus will serve as a witness against them for their earlier rejection or acceptance of his message.

questions.q: What did some Christians wrongly claim about Allah?
opts: B. That He is the third of three (Trinity)
C. That He has no partners
D. That He is the Creator
questions.explanation: Some Christians wrongly claimed that Allah is part of a Trinity, meaning He is one of three persons in one God. However, Islamic belief emphasizes the absolute oneness of Allah, rejecting any division or partnership in His nature.

questions.q: What did Allah say about those who claim He is the Messiah, son of Mary?
questions.explanation: Claiming that Allah is the Messiah, son of Mary, contradicts the core Islamic belief in the oneness of Allah (Tawhid). Such a claim attributes divinity to a human, which is considered disbelief (kufr) in Islam.

questions.q: What did Allah say about those who say "Allah is the third of the three"?
questions.explanation: Saying "Allah is the third of the three" contradicts the fundamental Islamic belief in the oneness of Allah (Tawhid), making it a form of shirk (associating partners with Allah), which leads to disbelief according to Islamic teachings.

questions.q: What did the Jews claim about Ezra (Uzair)?
opts: A. That he was a prophet
B. That he was the son of Allah
C. That he was a king
D. That he was an angel
questions.explanation: Some Jews claimed that Ezra (Uzair) was the son of Allah, a belief mentioned in the Quran to highlight incorrect attributions of divinity to prophets. Islam emphasizes that Allah has no offspring, and such claims are considered false.

questions.q: How does Surah Al-Ikhlas describe Allah?
opts: A. He is One, As-Samad, He begets not nor was He begotten, and none is like Him
B. He is the Father and the Son
C. He is three in one
questions.explanation: Surah Al-Ikhlas emphasizes Allah's absolute oneness (Tawhid), describing Him as As-Samad (the Self-Sufficient), who neither begets nor is begotten, highlighting that He has no offspring or parents, and that nothing is comparable to Him, affirming His unique and unparalleled nature.

questions.q: What did Jesus say about his nature according to the Quran?
opts: B. "Verily, I am a slave of Allah"
C. "I am the Son of God"
D. "I and the Father are one"
questions.explanation: Jesus ﷺ emphasized his servitude to Allah by stating, "Verily, I am a slave of Allah," highlighting his role as a devoted servant and messenger rather than divine himself, according to the Quran. This underscores the Islamic belief in the strict monotheism and prophethood of Jesus ﷺ.

questions.q: According to the Quran, who were the disciples of Jesus?
opts: B. They were his helpers in Allah's cause who believed in Allah and bore witness that they were Muslims
questions.explanation: The disciples of Jesus (Isa ﷺ) are described in the Quran as his devoted followers who supported his mission, believed in Allah, and identified themselves as Muslims, meaning those who submit to Allah's will. Their faith and commitment exemplify true discipleship in Islam.

questions.q: What was the primary purpose and goal of all prophets?
opts: B. To save people from misguidance and lead them from darkness to light
questions.explanation: All prophets were sent by Allah to guide humanity away from misguidance and sin, leading them towards faith, righteousness, and enlightenment. Their mission was to bring people from spiritual darkness into the light of true knowledge and worship of the One God.

questions.q: What is the most important and central call of all prophets?
opts: C. To build places of worship
questions.explanation: All prophets were sent with the primary mission to call people to worship Allaah alone, emphasizing Tawheed, which means recognizing and affirming the oneness of Allaah without associating any partners with Him. This is the foundation of Islamic belief and the core message of all prophetic teachings.

questions.q: What does "Tawheed" mean?
opts: B. Giving charity to the poor
C. Believing in the Oneness of Allaah and worshipping Him alone
questions.explanation: Tawheed means recognizing and affirming that Allaah is One without any partners, and directing all worship exclusively to Him. It is the fundamental concept in Islam emphasizing monotheism and the exclusive right of Allaah to be worshipped.

questions.q: What two things did the prophets bring to humanity?
opts: B. Glad news (for believers) and warning (from punishment)
questions.explanation: Prophets brought glad news to encourage and reward believers, and warnings to alert people about the consequences of disbelief and wrongdoing, guiding humanity towards righteousness and salvation.

questions.q: Why did Allaah send messengers with glad news and warnings?
opts: B. So that people would have no argument against Allaah after the messengers
D. To fulfill an ancient promise
questions.explanation: Allaah sent messengers with glad news and warnings to clearly convey His guidance and expectations, ensuring people are fully informed. This removes any excuse or argument against Allaah on the Day of Judgment, as everyone has received the message through His prophets.

questions.q: What did the prophets bring to establish justice among people?
questions.explanation: Prophets brought scripture, which contains divine guidance, and the "balance" (law) to establish fairness and justice among people. These tools help regulate behavior, ensuring rights are upheld and wrongs are corrected in society.

questions.q: How did the prophets act as excellent examples for humanity?
opts: C. They had the best character and guidance
questions.explanation: Prophets acted as excellent examples because they embodied the highest moral character and followed divine guidance perfectly, demonstrating how to live righteously and fulfill God's commands. Their behavior serves as a model for humanity to emulate in faith and conduct.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, what did the prophets ask Allaah for?
questions.explanation: Prophets asked Allah for a major acceptable supplication (da'wah) to ensure their prayers and requests were accepted, reflecting their deep reliance on Allah’s mercy and guidance in fulfilling their mission. This highlights the importance of sincere and effective supplication in prophetic teachings.

questions.q: Did the prophets ask their people for material rewards?
opts: A. Yes, they asked for money
B. Yes, they asked for food and shelter
C. No, they never asked for any reward; their reward was from Allaah
D. Sometimes they asked, sometimes they didn't
questions.explanation: Prophets did not seek material rewards because their mission was to guide people sincerely for the sake of Allah alone. Their true reward is the spiritual fulfillment and blessings granted by Allah, not worldly gain.

questions.q: What is a "miracle" according to the text?
opts: B. An exceptional power beyond human ability given by Allaah to prophets
questions.explanation: A miracle is an extraordinary event or ability granted by Allaah to prophets, demonstrating divine support and confirming their truthfulness, which surpasses normal human capabilities. This distinguishes prophets and validates their message.

questions.q: What does "'ismah" mean in relation to the prophets?
opts: A. They never made any mistakes at all
B. Protection from making mistakes in delivering Allaah's message
C. They were invisible to their enemies
questions.explanation: 'Ismah' refers to the divine protection granted to prophets, ensuring they do not make errors or mistakes when conveying Allah's message, thus preserving the purity and accuracy of the revelation.

questions.q: What did Allaah promise His messengers regarding victory?
opts: A. They would always win every battle
B. They and those who believed in them would have victory in this life and the Hereafter
C. They would never face opposition
questions.explanation: Allaah promised His messengers that both they and their followers would achieve success and triumph not only in this worldly life but also in the Hereafter, ensuring ultimate spiritual and moral victory through faith and perseverance.

questions.q: Who will be the first to intercede (shafaa'ah) on Judgment Day?
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) will be the first to intercede on Judgment Day because Allah granted him a special status among all prophets, allowing him to plead on behalf of believers for mercy and forgiveness. This honor highlights his unique role as the final messenger.

questions.q: What did Nooh (Noah) pray against his people?
opts: A. That they would become rich
B. That not a single disbeliever be left on earth
C. That they would be forgiven
D. That they would accept Islam
questions.explanation: Nooh (Noah) prayed that no disbeliever would remain on earth because his people persistently rejected his message and continued in disbelief, leading to their destruction by the flood as a divine punishment. This prayer reflects his desire for a purified, faithful community.

questions.q: Who chooses who will be a prophet?
opts: A. The people elect the most righteous among them
B. The previous prophet chooses his successor
C. Allaah chooses whom He wills
D. The angels select the best human
questions.explanation: Prophethood is a divine appointment, and only Allaah has the authority to select His prophets. He chooses individuals based on His wisdom and knowledge, guiding them to deliver His message to humanity.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, prophets are like brothers from one father but different mothers. What does this mean?
opts: A. They all had the same father but different mothers
B. Their religion (deen) is one, but their laws (sharī'ah) differ
C. They are all blood relatives
questions.explanation: This means all prophets share the same fundamental message of monotheism and submission to Allah, representing one unified religion, but they were sent to different peoples with distinct laws and practices suited to their specific communities.

questions.q: What does "Sidq" (truthfulness) mean in relation to the prophets?
opts: B. They were always truthful in what they said and conveyed
C. They were serious and never joked
questions.explanation: Sidq means that prophets consistently spoke the truth and faithfully conveyed Allah’s message without any falsehood or deceit. Their honesty ensured that their teachings were reliable and trustworthy for guiding people.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, what was special about the prophets' hearts while they slept?
opts: B. Their hearts did not sleep (remained alert)
C. They slept more than others
D. They only slept standing up
questions.explanation: Prophets' hearts did not sleep, meaning they remained spiritually alert and connected to Allah even during physical rest. This special state allowed them to receive divine guidance and maintain constant awareness of their mission.

questions.q: According to the text, are the prophets the best of humans?
opts: B. Yes, they are the best of humans
C. Some are better, some are worse
D. Only the messengers are the best
questions.explanation: Prophets are considered the best of humans because they are chosen by Allah for their exemplary character, wisdom, and unwavering faith. They serve as perfect role models, guiding humanity with divine revelation and moral integrity.

questions.q: Why were all prophets men and not women?
opts: A. Women are not righteous enough
B. Prophets needed to do things better suited for men (public speaking, leading armies, etc.)
C. Allaah does not love women as much
questions.explanation: Prophets were chosen as men because their roles often involved public leadership, delivering messages openly, and leading communities or armies, tasks traditionally suited to men in that historical and social context. This does not diminish the spiritual status of women but reflects societal roles at the time.

questions.q: Did the prophets eat, drink, and have families like other humans?
opts: A. No, they were angels in human form
B. Yes, they ate, drank, married, and had children
C. They ate but did not marry
D. They married but did not eat
questions.explanation: Prophets were human beings who experienced normal human needs and relationships, including eating, drinking, marrying, and having children, to demonstrate that they lived relatable lives while conveying God's message. This emphasizes their humanity and helps people connect with their teachings.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, what profession did most prophets practice?
questions.explanation: Most prophets practiced shepherding because it taught patience, responsibility, and care—qualities essential for leadership and guiding people. This humble profession also allowed them time for reflection and connection with nature, preparing them for their prophetic missions.

questions.q: What profession did Zakariyyaa (Zechariah) practice?
questions.explanation: Zakariyyaa (Zechariah) was known as a carpenter, a profession involving skilled woodwork. This trade reflects his humble and diligent character, emphasizing the prophets' connection to everyday life and honest labor.

questions.q: Did the prophets know the unseen (ghayb)?
opts: B. No, they only knew what Allaah told them
C. They knew the future but not the past
questions.explanation: Prophets do not have independent knowledge of the unseen (ghayb); they only know what Allah reveals to them through divine revelation. Their knowledge is limited to what Allah chooses to inform them, ensuring their messages are truthful and guided.

questions.q: According to the text, what does 'ismah NOT mean?
opts: A. The prophets never made mistakes in delivering the message
B. The prophets never committed any sin or mistake at all
C. Allaah protected them from major sins
D. They were guided in their mission
questions.explanation: 'Ismah' means that prophets are protected by Allah from major sins and errors in conveying the message, but it does not imply they never made any minor mistakes or human errors. They are safeguarded in their prophetic duties, ensuring the message remains pure and trustworthy.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, how are prophets tried (tested) compared to others?
opts: A. They are tried less than others
B. They are tried harder than others, and their rewards are multiplied
C. They are not tried at all
D. They are tried the same as everyone else
questions.explanation: Prophets are tested more severely than others because they bear greater responsibilities in guiding people. Their trials are more intense, but their rewards are also multiplied, reflecting their elevated status and the importance of their mission.

questions.q: What happens to the bodies of prophets after death?
opts: A. They rot and decompose like everyone else
B. They do not rot; Allaah has forbidden the earth to eat their bodies
C. They turn to dust immediately
D. They are taken to heaven
questions.explanation: Prophets' bodies do not decay after death because Allah has forbidden the earth from consuming them, preserving their purity and honor. This special protection reflects their elevated status and the respect Allah grants to His chosen messengers.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, what do prophets do in their graves?
opts: A. They sleep until Judgment Day
B. They are alive and praying
questions.explanation: Prophets remain alive in their graves, a state known as "life of the grave," where they continue to worship and pray to Allah. This special status reflects their unique closeness to God and ongoing spiritual role even after death.

questions.q: Why did Allaah send prophets speaking the language of their people?
opts: A. To make it easier for them to learn new languages
B. To make the message clear and understandable
C. Because prophets only knew one language
D. To show off their linguistic skills
questions.explanation: Prophets were sent speaking the language of their people to ensure the divine message was easily understood and accessible, allowing people to grasp guidance without language barriers. This facilitated effective communication and strengthened the impact of the teachings.

questions.q: According to the Prophet, how much beauty was Yoosuf (Joseph) given?
opts: A. All of the beauty in the world
C. The most beauty among prophets
questions.explanation: Prophet Yoosuf (Joseph) was given half of all beauty, highlighting his exceptional physical attractiveness as mentioned in Islamic tradition. This emphasizes his unique charm and the special qualities bestowed upon him by Allah.

questions.q: Which prophet did Allaah speak to directly?
questions.explanation: Allaah spoke directly to Prophet Moosaa (Moses) on Mount Sinai, a unique communication known as "Kalam" (direct speech). This direct dialogue distinguishes him among the prophets, as most received revelation through angels.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, which prophet will cling to the leg of the Throne on the Day when people are shocked?
questions.explanation: Prophet Moosaa (Moses) is described in a hadeeth as clinging to the leg of the Throne on the Day of Judgment, symbolizing his unique closeness to Allah and his role in interceding for people during a time of great fear and shock.

questions.q: What did the People of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) worship besides Allaah?
opts: B. Their rabbis and monks as lords
questions.explanation: Some People of the Scripture showed excessive reverence by elevating their rabbis and monks to a status resembling lords, which diverted their worship from Allah alone. Islam teaches that worship is due to Allah only, without associating partners or intermediaries.

questions.q: According to the text, who is the best of all prophets?
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad is considered the best of all prophets because he is the final messenger sent by Allah, completing the message of previous prophets and serving as the perfect example of moral and spiritual conduct for all humanity.

questions.q: Which prophet will be the first to come out of the grave on Judgment Day?
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) is believed to be the first to be resurrected on Judgment Day because of his status as the final and greatest prophet, serving as a leader and intercessor for humanity on that day. This highlights his unique role in Islamic eschatology.

questions.q: According to the hadeeth, which prophet was given a miracle that no one from his entire nation believed in him except one man?
questions.explanation: Prophet Looṭ (Lot) was given the miracle of the destruction of his people due to their sinful behavior, but only one man from his nation believed in him. This highlights the challenge prophets faced in guiding their communities toward faith and righteousness despite widespread disbelief.

questions.q: What is our duty toward the commands of the prophets?
opts: A. We can choose to follow them or not
